body:
This fixes the performance issues in ClimaShallowWater.jl

title: Disable bounds checking for gpu spectral element operators

body:
This [build](https://buildkite.com/clima/climacore-ci/builds/1925#01877d24-260a-429a-9fad-12761524528e) broke and did not touch the src code, so the axis tensor tests are either broken (axis tensor conversions seem more sparse now) or flakey somehow.

This PR:
 - Trims whitespaces in `method_info`
 - Adds a flag for conveniently printing entries of `method_info`

If this happens again, it should be easy enough to update the `method_info` entries
title: Make updating axis tensor tests easier

body:
This PR adds spectral operator benchmarks for cuda kernels. Some of this was ported over from ClimaShallowWater for convenience.

I think the simplest thing to do for now is to compare these kernels with a `copyto!` with the same size data-- this should provide a very rough estimate of each kernel's performance.


This is what the output looks like

```julia
julia> using Revise; using ClimaCore; include(joinpath(pkgdir(ClimaCore), "test", "Operators", "spectralelement", "benchmark_cuda.jl"))
┌ Info: Setting up benchmark
│   device = ClimaComms.CPU()
│   context = ClimaComms.SingletonCommsContext{ClimaComms.CPU}(ClimaComms.CPU())
│   float_type = Float64
│   panel_size = 8
└   poly_nodes = 4
[ Info: Benchmarking kernel_spectral_wdiv!...
[ Info: Benchmarking kernel_spectral_grad!...
[ Info: Benchmarking kernel_spectral_grad_norm!...
[ Info: Benchmarking kernel_spectral_div_grad!...
[ Info: Benchmarking kernel_spectral_wgrad_div!...
[ Info: Benchmarking kernel_spectral_wcurl_curl!...
[ Info: Benchmarking kernel_spectral_u_cross_curl_u!...
┌─────────────────────────────────┬────────────┬───────────┬───────────┐
│ Operator                        │  Mean time │ Mean time │ N-samples │
│                                 │  ClimaCore │     Array │           │
├─────────────────────────────────┼────────────┼───────────┼───────────┤
│ kernel_spectral_wdiv!           │  82.002 μs │  1.070 μs │     10000 │
│ kernel_spectral_grad!           │  77.473 μs │  1.080 μs │     10000 │
│ kernel_spectral_grad_norm!      │ 102.390 μs │  1.056 μs │     10000 │
│ kernel_spectral_div_grad!       │ 149.563 μs │  1.065 μs │     10000 │
│ kernel_spectral_wgrad_div!      │ 170.619 μs │  1.064 μs │     10000 │
│ kernel_spectral_wcurl_curl!     │ 186.472 μs │  1.082 μs │     10000 │
│ kernel_spectral_u_cross_curl_u! │  99.879 μs │  1.069 μs │     10000 │
└─────────────────────────────────┴────────────┴───────────┴───────────┘
    best_times[:kernel_spectral_wdiv!] = 82002.2282
    best_times[:kernel_spectral_grad!] = 77473.0846
    best_times[:kernel_spectral_grad_norm!] = 102389.9114
    best_times[:kernel_spectral_div_grad!] = 149562.6584
    best_times[:kernel_spectral_wgrad_div!] = 170619.4726
    best_times[:kernel_spectral_wcurl_curl!] = 186471.8404
    best_times[:kernel_spectral_u_cross_curl_u!] = 99878.7363
[ Info: Spectral element CUDA operator benchmarks passed 🎉
```

The new table, with changes in #1186, is:

```
┌─────────────────────────────────┬───────────┬───────────┬───────────┐
│ Operator                        │ Mean time │ Mean time │ N-samples │
│                                 │ ClimaCore │     Array │           │
├─────────────────────────────────┼───────────┼───────────┼───────────┤
│ kernel_spectral_wdiv!           │ 21.999 μs │ 67.361 μs │     10000 │
│ kernel_spectral_grad!           │ 22.262 μs │ 67.415 μs │     10000 │
│ kernel_spectral_grad_norm!      │ 52.984 μs │ 67.599 μs │     10000 │
│ kernel_spectral_div_grad!       │ 23.876 μs │ 67.305 μs │     10000 │
│ kernel_spectral_wgrad_div!      │ 25.003 μs │ 67.381 μs │     10000 │
│ kernel_spectral_wcurl_curl!     │ 75.570 μs │ 67.219 μs │     10000 │
│ kernel_spectral_u_cross_curl_u! │ 35.943 μs │ 67.032 μs │     10000 │
└─────────────────────────────────┴───────────┴───────────┴───────────┘
```
title: Add spectral operator benchmarks for cuda kernel

body: This PR extends the spectral element benchmarks to include: CPU / CUDA, Float32 / Float64.
title: Extend spectral element benchmarks
